mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate persistently below 0.25% anchors the risk-free rate at the zero lower bound, compressing equity discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad demand-supply expansion that amplifies earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns only when both series jointly increase.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of RSAFS and INDPRO month-over-month changes produces a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate sustained above 6.5% indicates persistent labor market slack that weakens aggregate demand and compresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates manufacturing sector strength that translates into higher near-term corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a near-zero federal funds rate creates deeply negative real interest rates that reduce real discount rates and drive equity valuations higher, requiring both conditions to hold.
failure_condition: Joint high CPIAUCSL growth and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP drives a repricing of corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when the economy is recovering from a prior growth slowdown.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter accelerations in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
